ads.author: Scott Nielsen
ads.title: Vintage Road Show Pinball Clock
ads.description: The image shows a novelty clock that appears to be themed around a pinball machine called "Road Show." This pinball machine is officially known as "Red & Ted's Road Show" and was released by Williams in 1994. The game features two talking heads, Red and Ted, who are road construction workers traveling across the United States. The image features characters, a bulldozer, and text elements similar to the artwork found on the actual pinball machine. One specific element in the image, "STIA NOV", likely refers to "Pinball," based on a popular pinball machine database website, where a machine from the company Bally is titled "Bally The Game Show Pinball Machine (Bally, 1990) - Pinside.com". However, the other text elements "ROAD SHOW" and other visual components indicate that the image is a novelty item related to the "Road Show" pinball machine. For more information please call or message me. Located in Spencer Tennessee [hidden information]

ads.author: Jeanni Perry
ads.title: Buttonbush, Cephalanthus occidentalis
ads.description: https://bellajardins.boutique/product/buttonbush/ My company is Bella Jardins Boutique, an on-line garden shop (no storefront) that specializes in Florida native plants. Orders are made on-line (copy/paste link above to your browser search) then I deliver the plants to you. Sales are limited to only these North Central Florida counties: Baker, Columbia, Union, Alachua, Bradford, Putnam, Clay, St. Johns, and Duval. Total orders that are over $25 before taxes have no delivery fee, orders that are under $25 have a $5 delivery fee. Botanical Name: Cephalanthus occidentalis Common Name: Buttonbush, Honeyballs, Spanish Pincushion, Globeflowers, Swampwood, Button-Willow, Crane Willow, Little Snowball, Pinball, Pond Dogwood Description: Buttonbush is another one of my favorite Florida native shrubs. The fragrant, white ball-shaped flowers look like pincushions and bloom Spring to Summer. In its native habitat, Buttonbush is a wetland shrub, but it adapts to regular garden environments as long as it gets water on a regular basis from either rain or irrigation. Its roots can withstand full submersion in wet areas. I wouldn’t call it a drought tolerant plant. Plant it in that rain garden you’ve been planning, or beside that downspout, or in that low area that stays moist where nothing else will grow, or at the edge of your pond, or in your regular garden beds that get irrigation. It’s fast growing, likes sun, part sun, or part shade, is deciduous, and grows 5 – 12′ tall x 4 – 8′ wide into a loose, rounded habit. The more shade it’s in, the more open the foliage will be. I’ve seen these growing in the wild, and they are glorious plants that are abuzz with butterflies, Titan sphinx moth, Hydrangea sphinx moth, hummers, native bees, honey bees, bumble bees, and all kinds of other pollinators when in bloom, and at least 25 species of birds eat the seeds! You need this shrub in your garden. Is not deer tolerant. Is host plant to several moths including titan sphinx (Aellopos titan) and hydrangea sphinx (Darapsa versicolor) moths. The foliage is poisonous to livestock and people if eaten. It grows in clay, loam, organic material (muck), and sandy soils that are acidic to calcareous. Not salt tolerant of inundation by salty or brackish water. Low/no tolerance of salty wind or direct salt spray. This plant in 3-gallon containers is 2-4′ tall. Plant Lore: The genus Cephalanthus is from the Greek words kephale, or “head,” and ánthos, or “flower.” The species epithet occidentalis is Latin for “west” or “western,” suggesting the plant is native to the western hemisphere. Florida Hardiness Zones 8 – 11

Easy in and out of building for loading. $1,500 Here's a summary of the game from AI. Crisis Zone (1999) is an intense, action-packed arcade light-gun shooter by Namco and a spin-off of the Time Crisis series. Players control Claude McGarren, an elite STF agent, utilizing a mounted submachine gun (Steyr Mannlicher TMP) and a foot pedal system to take cover, reload, and destroy massive, highly destructible environments while battling terrorists in a 3D urban setting. Key Features and Gameplay: Unique Controller: Instead of a pistol, players use a mounted submachine gun with 40-round capacity and a laser sight. Cover Mechanism: A foot pedal allows players to take cover, acting as both a reload function and a shield against enemy fire. Destructible Environments: Players can destroy the environment, including shooting CDs off shelves, breaking glass, and destroying clothing racks. Campaign Structure: The game features three main stages (Subway, Hotel, Commercial Zone) that can be played in any order, followed by a final boss stage. Action-Oriented: The game is known for high-intensity, rapid-fire gameplay, often featuring faster enemy encounters than the standard Time Crisis. Crisis Zone is powered by Namco System 23 hardware and typically features 27-inch CRT or 50-inch projection displays.
